title: Edit Profile: nested form detaches Save button
description: A nested <form> for the notification-level selector (added by c43bcf5) sits inside #edit-profile-form. Invalid HTML: the browser closes the profile form early at the inner </form>, detaching the Save submit button. Result: save_profile never fires, so status doesn't persist and the modal never dismisses. Fix: replace nested <form> with a bare <select phx-change=update_notification_level>. Regression test added (only render_submit-by-id bypassed it, plus :push_notifications off in test).
close_reason: Fixed in ddb521b — unnested notification-level form; regression test added

title: Bump GitHub Actions off deprecated Node 20 runtime before forced Node 24 cutover
description: GitHub is forcing all JavaScript actions to Node 24 on 2026-06-16; Node 20 is removed from runners on 2026-09-16. CI annotations on the v0.9.25 deploy run (26983946203) flagged these Node-20 actions in .github/workflows/ci-deploy.yml: actions/checkout@v4, actions/cache@v4, docker/build-push-action@v6, docker/login-action@v3, docker/setup-buildx-action@v3. Pin/upgrade each to a release that ships a Node 24 entrypoint (or set FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true as a stopgap). Verify a tag deploy still goes green after the bump. Ref: https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/
acceptance_criteria: All JS-based actions in .github/workflows/ run on Node 24; no Node-20 deprecation annotations on a fresh CI run; a tag deploy completes successfully.
close_reason: Bumped all flagged actions to Node 24 majors (checkout@v5, cache@v5, setup-buildx@v4, login@v4, build-push@v7). Master Quality run 27023221834 and v0.9.26 deploy run 27023635535 both green with zero Node-20 deprecation annotations; full tag deploy validated end-to-end.

title: PWA chrome: purple -> Loom black/gold
description: PWA chrome (manifest theme/background colors, theme-color meta tags, offline page, app icons) still uses the pre-Loom purple (#5D4D8F / #2A2640 / #E8A835). Update to the warm-charcoal+gold Loom palette: surfaces #131109 (header) and #0b0a07 (body splash), light surface #fbf7eb, gold accent #e8c547 on the icon mark only. CRITICAL: bump service-worker CACHE_NAME (v3->v4) so existing installs reinstall and pick up the new offline page. Regenerate the 3 purple PNG app icons to charcoal bg + gold serif T.
close_reason: PWA chrome restyled to Loom black/gold; icons+favicon regenerated; SW cache bumped

title: Decompose ChatLive.Index god-LiveView by concern (on_mount + attach_hook)
description: lib/slackex_web/live/chat_live/index.ex is a god-LiveView: 48 handle_event + 39 handle_info + 8 handle_params = 95 callback clauses, ~45 assigns in mount, spanning ~14 distinct feature concerns (messages, threads, reactions, search, summary streaming, DM-safety/reports, profiles, push, presence/typing, channels, quick-switcher, appearance, sous cards, catchup). Clause-level code is good (delegates to Chat/Accounts/Helpers) — the problem is concern concentration in one module. The 'extracted to keep cyclomatic complexity within credo budget' comment on dispatch_message is the tell: complexity pressure escaped sideways into more functions in the same file because module-level decomposition is overdue. Tier 1 (extract push-notification DeviceToken persistence + health derivation into Slackex.Notifications.DeviceTokens / PushHealth) is DONE — this issue tracks Tier 2/3.
design: Tier 2 (idiomatic, behavior-preserving): split callbacks by concern using on_mount + Phoenix.LiveView.attach_hook(:handle_event|:handle_info). Each concern becomes a module with on_mount/4 that assigns its defaults and attaches hooks returning {:halt, socket} when it handles the event/msg, {:cont, socket} to pass through. Proposed grouping (14->7): Messages (stays in LiveView: send/edit/delete/pin/reaction/load_more + message.new/edited + link previews), PushNotifications (9 push events + infos), Profiles, DmSafety (accept/decline/block + report modal), SearchPanel, Summary (streaming infos), PresenceTyping (typing/heartbeats/presence). Quick-switcher/appearance/sidebar/sous fold into nearest or a small Chrome hook. Do Summary or PushNotifications first as the pattern-setter, then the rest is mechanical. Tier 3: promote remaining inline modals (report, edit-profile, appearance) to self-contained LiveComponents owning their own assigns+events (search/thread/sidebar already are), which removes those assigns from mount.
acceptance_criteria: index.ex reduced to mount skeleton + message pipeline + render; each extracted concern is its own module with its assigns/events/infos; full chat_live suite stays green (behavior-preserving); mount assign count materially reduced.

title: Architecture docs: fill two deferred completeness gaps
description: The comprehensive architecture doc set (docs/architecture/, 29 docs) deferred two minor coverage gaps flagged by the completeness critic. Both are partially covered elsewhere; standalone docs would aid discovery. (1) Infrastructure utilities: lib/slackex/infrastructure/rate_limiter.ex has no dedicated coverage (snowflake.ex is fully covered by deep-dive-snowflake-partitioning.md). (2) A dedicated lib/slackex/pipeline/ doc for BatchWriter lifecycle/writer-epoch fencing — currently split across message-pipeline-and-persistence.md, deep-dive-multi-node-horde.md, and deep-dive-pipeline-events-bridge.md. Decision to defer: content is not missing, only un-consolidated; the workers gap (the third critic finding) WAS filled with background-jobs-and-workers.md.
acceptance_criteria: Either a rate-limiter section/doc exists, or README explicitly notes infrastructure utilities live in cross-cutting/deep-dive docs; pipeline/BatchWriter has a clear single home or an explicit cross-reference note.

title: Sous: let a viewer override the AI-generated Prism facet text
description: The Facet Drawer's per-role Prism text is AI-generated (Slice B2). It should be clearer in the UI that this text is AI-generated, and a viewer should be able to override/edit it with their own wording — the human has final say over how a decision is framed for their role. Currently set_facet_text/3 is the only write path and facet_text comes solely from FacetWorker (:facet_generated event). A human override is a distinct path.
design: Add a :facet_overridden event type + extend set_facet_text (or a sibling command) so a viewer-authored facet_text wins over the AI one and is NOT re-staled/regenerated by prompt_version bumps or :state_changed (an override is sticky until the human clears it). UI: label the drawer text as AI-generated (e.g. a small 'AI' chip + 'edit' affordance); on edit, persist as override. Pill-state derivation (WorkItemFacet.state/1) needs an :overridden state so it renders distinctly from fresh/stale/generating. Fits the B-later bucket noted in slice-b2 design doc §2 (alongside role-management UI and per-user personalisation).
acceptance_criteria: A viewer can edit a Prism facet; the edited text persists and survives a prompt_version bump and state changes; the UI distinguishes AI-generated vs human-overridden; clearing an override returns the row to AI regeneration.
